simple.title: Buffer Critically Low
simple.description: Your video might freeze soon. Less than 2 seconds of video is ready to play.
actions: Pause the video for 10-20 seconds to let it load more
Lower the video quality if possible
Close other apps or browser tabs using internet
Try refreshing the page if nothing helps

technical.title: Buffer Critically Low
technical.description: Buffer level < 2s. Rebuffering event imminent. Insufficient pre-loaded content to maintain continuous playback.
actions: Pause playback for 10-20s to rebuild buffer depth
Reduce quality tier (ABR manual override)
Terminate concurrent bandwidth-intensive processes
Perform hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) to re-establish CDN connection

simple.title: Recent Playback Stall
simple.description: Your video froze recently ({secs}s ago). You've had {stallCount} freeze(s) this session.
actions: Lower the video quality
Close other apps using internet
Check if a VPN or proxy is slowing your connection
Try using a wired connection instead of Wi-Fi

technical.title: Recent Playback Stall
technical.description: Stall detected {secs}s ago. Session stall count: {stallCount}. Indicates network congestion or decode instability.
actions: Decrease quality tier to reduce bitrate requirements
Close background processes consuming bandwidth
Verify VPN/proxy is not throttling throughput
Switch to Ethernet to eliminate Wi-Fi latency variability

simple.title: Not Enough Internet Speed
simple.description: The video needs {bitrate} kbps but you only have {bandwidth} kbps. Missing {deficit} kbps.
actions: Choose a lower quality (like 720p instead of 1080p)
Stop any downloads or uploads
Check if your internet provider is having issues

technical.title: Bandwidth Insufficient for Current Bitrate
technical.description: Stream bitrate: ~{bitrate} kbps. Available bandwidth: ~{bandwidth} kbps. Deficit: {deficit} kbps. High rebuffering probability.
actions: Select lower quality tier (1080p → 720p → 480p)
Suspend concurrent uploads/downloads
Monitor ISP congestion status via network diagnostics

simple.title: High Frame Drop Rate
simple.description: Your device is dropping {dropRate}% of video frames. This causes stuttering and choppy video.
actions: Close games or other heavy programs
Turn off hardware acceleration in browser settings and restart
Lower the video resolution
Update your graphics drivers

technical.title: High Frame Drop Rate
technical.description: Frame drop rate: {dropRate}%. Indicates GPU decode bottleneck or system resource contention. Observable frame timing jitter.
actions: Terminate GPU-intensive processes (3D rendering, gaming)
Disable hardware acceleration (browser settings → restart required)
Reduce decode workload via resolution downgrade
Update GPU drivers to latest stable release

simple.title: Buffer Running Low
simple.description: Only {buffer}s of video is loaded ahead. If your internet slows down, the video might freeze.
actions: Pause for a few seconds to let more video load
Consider lowering the quality by one level

technical.title: Buffer Running Low
technical.description: Buffer depth: {buffer}s. Below optimal threshold (8s+). Vulnerable to network condition degradation.
actions: Pause temporarily to increase buffer depth
Reduce quality tier by one level to improve buffer fill rate

simple.title: Unstable Video Quality
simple.description: The video quality keeps changing frequently. This is usually caused by unstable internet connection.
actions: Lock the video to a specific quality if your player allows it
Move closer to your Wi-Fi router or use a cable
Try a different server if the platform supports it

technical.title: Bitrate Instability
technical.description: Significant bitrate variance detected. ABR algorithm performing frequent quality tier switches. Observable quality oscillation.
actions: Disable ABR and pin quality to fixed tier (manual override)
Reduce Wi-Fi path loss (proximity to AP) or switch to Ethernet
Select alternate CDN region if platform supports edge selection

simple.title: Slow Frame Processing
simple.description: Your device is taking {decodeTime}ms to process each frame. This is too slow and causes stuttering.
actions: Turn on hardware acceleration in browser settings
Lower the video resolution
Close other browser tabs and programs

technical.title: Decode Performance Degraded
technical.description: Average decode time: {decodeTime}ms. Exceeds real-time budget for smooth playback. Potential frame timing violations.
actions: Enable hardware-accelerated decode (browser settings)
Reduce decode complexity via resolution downgrade
Terminate concurrent browser tabs and background processes

simple.title: Multiple Freezes
simple.description: You've had {stallCount} freezes this session, totaling {stallDuration}s of lost time. There's likely a persistent issue.
actions: Test your internet speed at fast.com or speedtest.net
Refresh the page to try a different server
Try switching between mobile data and Wi-Fi to find the issue

technical.title: Recurring Stall Pattern
technical.description: Session stall count: {stallCount}. Total stall duration: {stallDuration}s. Indicates persistent network or origin server issue.
actions: Perform bandwidth diagnostic (fast.com / speedtest.net)
Hard refresh to re-establish CDN connection
Switch network interface (cellular ↔ Wi-Fi) to isolate issue

simple.title: Playback Stable
simple.description: Everything looks good. Your video is playing smoothly with no issues detected.

technical.title: Optimal Playback Conditions
technical.description: All quality metrics within acceptable thresholds. No corrective action required.

simple.title: Player Not Ready
simple.description: The video player is loading (state {readyState}). It doesn't have enough data to start playing yet.
actions: Wait for the initial loading to complete
If it's stuck for more than 15 seconds, try refreshing

technical.title: Insufficient ReadyState
technical.description: Media element readyState: {readyState}. Below HAVE_FUTURE_DATA (3). Waiting for initial buffer fill.
actions: Allow initial buffer fill to complete
Hard refresh if state remains < 3 for >15s

simple.title: Fast Playback Speed
simple.description: You're playing at {rate}× speed. Quality scores may be lower because the video system isn't optimized for fast playback.
actions: Keep in mind that stability scores during fast playback may not reflect normal performance

technical.title: Non-Standard Playback Rate
technical.description: Playback rate: {rate}×. Buffering strategies not calibrated for accelerated playback. Metrics may deviate from nominal operation.
actions: Interpret stability scores at rates >1.5× with caution (buffering heuristics optimized for 1.0×)

simple.title: High Live Stream Delay
simple.description: You have {buffer}s of buffer on a live stream. You're watching significantly behind the actual live broadcast.
actions: Click the 'go to live' button if available
Refresh the page if there's no live sync button

technical.title: Elevated Live Stream Latency
technical.description: Live stream buffer depth: {buffer}s. Viewing position significantly behind live edge. High glass-to-glass latency.
actions: Execute live edge re-synchronization (player UI 'go to live' control)
Hard refresh if live sync control unavailable
